evidence_quotes.quote: The next generation of game consoles are unlikely to support physical discs or cartridges, but will soon only be able to buy their games in digital marketplaces.
Consumers now buy a licence to play as long as the company supports the game on their platforms/servers.
If you choose to close down your servers, you lose access to something you have bought in good faith and there is currently nothing to protect consumers in these cases.

It shall be possible for consumers to play in offline mode if they own the game physically or have it downloaded locally, and avoid verification, or even be able to delete the account completely if they so wish, while retaining the games you have bought on the platform.

paragraphs.text: In addition to the very good proposals that already exist, I would like to add protection for consumers buying goods and services online, in particular games, but also films and books, to ensure that you always have access to what you have bought. The next generation of game consoles are unlikely to support physical discs or cartridges, but will soon only be able to buy their games in digital marketplaces. Consumers now buy a licence to play as long as the company supports the game on their platforms/servers. If you choose to close down your servers, you lose access to something you have bought in good faith and there is currently nothing to protect consumers in these cases. In addition, I also wish to introduce legislation to prevent unnecessary verification of user accounts when you want to play something that lacks online functionality. Some gambling companies force users to log in to play games, e.g. EA with The Sims 3 and 4, or Sony, which requires verification of your console via Playstation Network even if you want to play something you own physically on a disc. It shall be possible for consumers to play in offline mode if they own the game physically or have it downloaded locally, and avoid verification, or even be able to delete the account completely if they so wish, while retaining the games you have bought on the platform. This would provide consumers with greater protection in the event that the company shut down completely as you can continue to play in the future even if the platform no longer exists.
